subject: Cryptography
title: Certificate Authorities and Domain Validation
description: Certificate Authorities (CAs) are trusted third parties that issue digital certificates used in HTTPS and other public-key infrastructure systems. This note explains how CAs validate control of a domain, how certificates bind public keys to domain names, and why hashes, digital signatures, certificate chains, and browser trust stores are central to TLS security.
objectives: Explain the role of Certificate Authorities in public-key infrastructure
Describe how domain validation proves control of a domain
Understand common validation methods such as DNS, HTTP, email, and TLS-based challenges
Explain how certificate chains connect a website certificate to a trusted root certificate
Identify important limitations and security risks in the CA model
prerequisites: Basic understanding of DNS and HTTPS
